column5: Data / Payload Exchanged
Scenario environment request: scenario descriptor, topology template, exercise parameters
Instantiated virtual replicas ready for exercise; environment state and event stream
SBOM / xBOM / CBOM or structured component inventory provided by DT, attached to an xBOMGuard Project/Release as BOM/Evidence.
column6: File upload / dashboard upload / /api/v1 BOM import where agreed
column7: CycloneDX JSON preferred; SPDX JSON secondary

column5: Topology and target definitions: IP ranges, service map, exposed ports, network segments

Prioritised software/component exposure summary and test-candidate context: affected component versions, CVEs, priority band, reason codes, dependency/blast-radius context and suggested testing focus areas.
column6: Dashboard/report export; file exchange; /api/v1 export where agreed for V1.
column7: Assessment JSON; CSV/PDF report; CycloneDX BOM/VDR/VEX where applicable.
column10: V1 output supports pentesting preparation (no mandatory real-time integration or direct scanner orchestration is assumed for V1)

column5: Validated pentesting findings and exploitability evidence: confirmed affected components/releases, CVE or vulnerability references, observed exploitability, severity, reproduction/evidence references and remediation notes where available.
column6: SARIF/file import and evidence upload for V1; /api/v1 findings/evidence API where agreed.
column7: SARIF JSON preferred for structured findings; PDF/JSON/logs/screenshots as supporting Evidence.
column10: xBOMGuard can ingest validated pentest results as Findings and Evidence linked to the relevant Project/Release/ComponentVersion. For V1, manual upload or structured file exchange is sufficient; direct API integration can be agreed later. Pentesting outputs inform validation and prioritisation but do not directly perform risk-acceptance decisions.

column5: Software/component exposure summary for in-scope projects/releases: affected component versions, vulnerabilities, xBOMGuard priority, dependency/blast-radius context and evidence references.
column10: Manual evidence upload or structured file exchange is sufficient for V1; direct REST integration is optional and subject to agreement.

column5: Vulnerability evidence: validated exploit findings, affected assets, CVSS scores, attack paths
column10: V1 provides software/component exposure intelligence, not a complete operational asset-risk inventory. Consumption by RAP can be manual or file/API-based if agreed.

column5: Threat context: active threat actors, TTPs mapped to asset types, campaign intelligence
Threat context for hypothesis generation: relevant threat actors, TTPs, IoCs, campaign notes and MITRE ATT&CK mappings.
column6: REST API (to be confirmed; could be manual file exchange for V1)
column7: STIX 2.1 where available; JSON/CSV/Markdown summaries; MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs
column10: THM uses CTI context out-of-band to prepare hunt hypotheses and Kestrel-compatible hunt packs. We have to decide if we want real-time CTI ingestion for V1.

column5: CTI feeds: detection-relevant IOCs, watchlists, Sigma rules enriched with threat context
Prioritised risk scenarios and exposure context to guide threat-hunting scope and hypothesis selection.
column6: Manual/file exchange for V1; optional API later if agreed
column10: For D3.1 (V1), risk context may be reviewed by users and manually reflected in hunt hypotheses.

column5: Risk propagation / asset context: asset criticality, risk scores, cascading risk paths
Detection context or historical alert/result summaries that may inform hunt refinement: alert type, affected asset, observed behaviour and evidence references.
column6: Manual/file exchange for V1; optional API later if agreed
column7: JSON/CSV/PDF alert summary; logs only if explicitly authorised and sanitised
column10: No direct telemetry integration is assumed for V1

column5: Telemetry source/access context for executing huntflows, where explicitly available and authorised.
column6: To be clarified / not assumed for V1
column7: Kestrel data-source configuration or query connector configuration; not STIX as default
column10: No live telemetry integration is committed by CLX for D3.1. Kestrel execution may be demonstrated with representative/offline data or partner-provided runtime context.

column5: Hunt execution results: matched behaviours, observed indicators, affected entities, result summary and supporting evidence.
column6: File/report export for V1; optional API later if agreed
column7: JSON/CSV/Markdown/PDF result summary; STIX only if mapping is agreed
column10: For V1, results can be reviewed by users and shared out-of-band to inform PRV tools/RMCM reporting.

column5: Kestrel-compatible hunt packs/huntbooks generated from exposure, risk and CTI/TTP context.
column7: Kestrel huntflow (.hf); Markdown/PDF explanation; optional JSON metadata
column10: Preferred V1 flow: THM prepares and exports hunt artefacts. Execution occurs in an external Kestrel runtime or representative environment; no embedded runtime commitment.

column5: Risk exercise scenarios: threat scenarios derived from risk model for use in training exercises
Detection rules / scenarios: Sigma/Wazuh rules and incident scenarios for Red/Blue exercises
Exercise outcomes: exercise telemetry, defender actions, detection performance data
Access and configuration: exercise selection, training requests, operator profiles
